sections.subtitle: Team Form and Recent Performances
sections.text: Manchester United have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, with a strong attacking output (11 goals scored). Wolverhampton, however, are in dismal shape, losing all of their last 5 matches and conceding 11 goals while scoring only 3. United's recent dominance over Wolves, including a 4-1 victory in their last head-to-head, further highlights their edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, Manchester United have had the upper hand with a 4-1 win on December 8, 2025, though Wolves managed a surprising 1-0 victory on April 20, 2025. United's attacking prowess was evident in their latest clash, with 4.24 expected goals compared to Wolves' 0.41. Historically, matches between these teams often see low-scoring outcomes, with under 2.5 goals in 7 of their last 9 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics and Key Insights
sections.text: Manchester United sit 6th in the league with 29 points, boasting 32 goals scored (3rd in the league) but conceding 28 (14th defensively). Wolverhampton are rock bottom at 20th with just 2 points, having scored a mere 10 goals while conceding 39, the worst defensive record in the league. United's superior possession (53.2%) and chance creation (50 big chances) contrast sharply with Wolves' struggles across all metrics.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Key Absences
sections.text: Manchester United will miss key players like Bruno Fernandes and Amad Diallo, which could impact their creativity, though players like Casemiro (4 goals) remain threats. Wolverhampton also have significant absences, including midfielders André and Jean-Ricner Bellegarde, further weakening their already struggling squad. United's depth should help them cope better with these absences.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given the stark contrast in form and quality, Manchester United are heavy favorites, backed by Sofascore's 81% home win probability. Wolves' inability to keep clean sheets (14 consecutive games) and United's tendency to score early (first to score in 4/5 recent games) suggest a comfortable win for the hosts. Expect United to dominate possession and create multiple scoring opportunities at Old Trafford.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Manchester United and Wolverhampton are level with a 1-1 scoreline at Old Trafford. Both teams have shown moments of quality, with Joshua Zirkzee opening the scoring for the home side and Ladislav Krejčí equalizing late in the half for the visitors. The game has been competitive, with possession slightly favoring Manchester United, while Wolverhampton have been more clinical with their shots on target. Neither team has been dominant defensively, leaving room for more goals in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Manchester United started strongly with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes, pushing forward with intent. However, Wolverhampton responded with a shift in momentum midway through the half, building pressure through a series of dangerous moves. The home side regained control with a sustained period of attacking play around the time of their goal, but the visitors finished the half on top with a late surge that culminated in their equalizer. Both teams have shown they can turn defense into attack quickly, setting up an intriguing second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Manchester United held a slight edge in possession and created more entries into the final third and penalty area, indicating a more aggressive territorial approach. However, they missed a big chance, which could have given them the lead. Wolverhampton, on the other hand, were more effective with their limited opportunities, converting one of their fewer shots into a goal. The away side also dominated aerial duels, which could be a factor in set-piece situations. Both teams showed balanced duel-winning stats, suggesting a physically even contest so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout performers
halftimeSections.text: For Manchester United, Joshua Zirkzee has been the standout, not only scoring the opener but also contributing defensively with clearances. His presence in the box will be crucial in the second half. On the Wolverhampton side, Ladislav Krejčí made his mark with a well-taken header to level the score, showing composure under pressure. Tolu Arokodare also posed a threat with key passes, hinting at potential danger if given space after the break. Both goalscorers will likely be focal points for their respective teams moving forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Manchester United may look to capitalize on their higher number of final third entries by increasing the tempo and focusing on creating more clear-cut chances. Their missed big chance in the first half suggests they need to be sharper in front of goal. Wolverhampton, encouraged by their late equalizer, might push for more counter-attacking opportunities while relying on their aerial strength to exploit set pieces. Defensive organization will be key for both sides, as neither has looked entirely secure at the back. Substitutions could play a role in shifting the balance if fatigue sets in.
